meaning: 罗浮宫[法国巴黎](或译卢浮宫现为国立美术博物馆) ;n.<英>=louver
examples: What does the scene at the Louvre demonstrate according to the author?
When he visited the Louvre he could spent as much as four hours sitting before a painting.
No visit to Paris is complete without a trip to the Louvre, one of the largest and most famous museums in the world.
The Louvre gets its particular character because it evolved into a museum rather than being designed as one.
The Louvre is open every day (except Tuesday) from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m.
